title: dracaena zeylanica
intro: Dracaena zeylanica, also known as the Ceylon cane or false palm, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, native to Sri Lanka and southern India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. In addition to its medicinal properties, Dracaena zeylanica is also known to have various health benefits, such as improving respiratory health and acting as an antioxidant. Dracaena zeylanica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
